definition: The monetary policy transmission mechanism is partially impaired by sovereign debt dynamics. Rate hikes generate a fiscal impulse (each 100bp adds ~$250-320B in debt service) that offsets 10-25% of contractionary intent. The Kalecki channel keeps financial conditions looser than the policy rate implies. Central banks face a three-body problem: inflation vs. financial stability vs. sovereign funding.
evidence: Self-referential interest rate channel is mechanically sound
2022-24 puzzle: tight rates but loose financial conditions explained by Kalecki-FCI link
Basel III creates regulatory crowding-in equivalent to 1940s financial repression (~$4T bank sovereign holdings)
Fiscal impulse offset magnitude: realistic bondholder MPC yields 10-25%, not 25-50%
open_questions: What is the precise MPC of sovereign bondholders (institutional, foreign central banks, HNW)?
Does the three-body problem produce decades of muddling through or eventual fracture?
How does the transmission impairment vary across central bank credibility levels (Fed > ECB > BoJ)?
